sourceMovie.title: The Lives of Others
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Lives of Others works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Supremacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lives of Others clicked for you, The Bourne Supremacy (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: M
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lives of Others clicked for you, M (1931) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lives of Others?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Supremacy, M, The Sum of All Fears—each shares DNA with The Lives of Others while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lives of Others?
faq.answer: The Lives of Others (2006) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lives of Others worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
